user_query: How do I configure CORS in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use CORSMiddleware from fastapi.middleware.cors. Add it with app.add_middleware(CORSMiddleware, allow_origins=[...], allow_credentials=True, allow_methods=["*"], allow_headers=["*"])

user_query: How do I install FastAPI?
ground_truth: Install with pip install fastapi. For production, also install uvicorn: pip install uvicorn. For all optional dependencies: pip install fastapi[all]

user_query: How do I add custom middleware in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Create a class that implements __init__ and __call__ methods, then use app.add_middleware(). The __call__ method receives request and call_next parameters, processes the request, and returns the response

user_query: How do I set up a custom exception handler in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use @app.exception_handler(SomeException) decorator. The handler takes request and exception parameters and returns a Response. For HTTPException, use @app.exception_handler(HTTPException)

user_query: How do I run background tasks in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use BackgroundTasks from fastapi. Add a parameter of type BackgroundTasks to your endpoint, then call tasks.add_task(function, *args). The task runs after the response is sent

user_query: How do I create a WebSocket endpoint in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use @app.websocket("/ws") decorator. The endpoint receives a WebSocket parameter. Call await websocket.accept() to accept the connection, then use await websocket.receive_text() and await websocket.send_text() for communication

user_query: How do I use dependency injection in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Define a callable (function or class) and use Depends() as a parameter default. FastAPI calls the dependency and injects its return value. Dependencies can have their own sub-dependencies

user_query: How does FastAPI compare to Flask?
ground_truth: FastAPI is faster (ASGI vs WSGI), has automatic OpenAPI docs, built-in validation with Pydantic, async support natively, and better performance. Flask is more mature with a larger plugin ecosystem

user_query: How do I set up OpenAPI metadata in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Pass parameters to FastAPI() constructor: title, description, version, openapi_tags, etc. Docs are auto-generated at /docs (Swagger) and /redoc (ReDoc)

user_query: How do I handle file uploads in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use UploadFile from fastapi and File from fastapi. The parameter type is UploadFile which provides async read/write methods. Use form data for the upload

user_query: How do I validate request bodies in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use Pydantic BaseModel classes as parameter types. FastAPI automatically validates the request body against the model schema and returns 422 with error details if validation fails

user_query: How do I use OAuth2 with FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use OAuth2PasswordBearer from fastapi.security as a dependency. Implement a /token endpoint that validates credentials and returns a JWT. Protect routes by adding the token dependency

user_query: What is the difference between APIRouter and FastAPI app?
ground_truth: FastAPI() is the main application. APIRouter is a sub-router for organizing routes into modules. Include routers with app.include_router(router, prefix="/api")

user_query: How do I paginate results in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Accept limit and offset query parameters. Default: limit=10, offset=0. Return a subset of results and optionally include total count in the response

user_query: How do I handle database sessions in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use a dependency that creates a session, yields it, and closes it after the request. Use @contextmanager or @asynccontextmanager. Common with SQLAlchemy: SessionLocal() in dependency

user_query: How do I run FastAPI in production?
ground_truth: Use uvicorn with gunicorn as a process manager: gunicorn -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker main:app. Set workers to 2-4 per CPU core. Use behind a reverse proxy like Nginx

user_query: How do I add logging to FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use Python's logging module. Configure logging at app startup with logging.basicConfig or dictConfig. FastAPI uses uvicorn's logging by default which can be configured via uvicorn.run(log_config=...)

user_query: How do I create a rate limiter in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Implement middleware or a dependency that tracks request counts per client (using cache, Redis, or database). Return 429 Too Many Requests when the limit is exceeded

user_query: How do I handle CORS errors in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Add CORSMiddleware with appropriate allow_origins list. For development, allow_origins=["*"]. For production, specify exact origins. CORS errors appear as browser console errors, not in the server logs

user_query: How do I return a file download in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use FileResponse from fastapi.responses. Pass the file path and media_type. Optionally set headers for Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=...

user_query: What is the lifespan protocol in FastAPI?
ground_truth: The lifespan protocol replaces the deprecated startup/shutdown events. Use @app.contextmanager("lifespan") to define startup code before yield and shutdown code after yield

user_query: How do I upgrade from FastAPI 0.100 to 0.110?
ground_truth: Check the release notes. Key changes may include Pydantic v2 migration requirements, lifespan protocol replacing startup/shutdown events, and changes to OpenAPI schema generation

user_query: How do I create sub-applications with FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use app.mount("/subpath", sub_app) where sub_app is another FastAPI instance. Mounted apps have their own OpenAPI docs and middleware. Use APIRouter for simpler route organization instead

user_query: How do I test FastAPI endpoints?
ground_truth: Use TestClient from fastapi.testclient (based on httpx). Create a test app instance and use client.get/post/put/delete to test endpoints. Assert on status code and response JSON

user_query: What is the difference between Query, Path, and Body in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Query extracts parameters from URL query string. Path extracts from URL path. Body extracts from request body. All provide validation like ge, le, min_length, max_length, regex patterns

user_query: How do I handle 404 errors in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Create a custom exception handler with @app.exception_handler(404) or @app.exception_handler(HTTPException). For a catch-all 404, override the default handler which returns JSON: {"detail": "Not Found"}

user_query: How do I add request validation middleware in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Create middleware that inspects incoming requests and returns early with an error response if validation fails. Use @app.middleware("http") for ASGI-level middleware that runs before route handlers

user_query: How do I use streaming responses in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use StreamingResponse from fastapi.responses. Pass an async generator or iterable as content. Set media_type as needed. Useful for large files or server-sent events

user_query: How do I configure HTTPS redirect in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use TrustedHostMiddleware and handle redirect in middleware, or use a reverse proxy (Nginx/Caddy) for SSL termination. FastAPI itself doesn't serve HTTPS directly; use uvicorn with SSL certs for development

user_query: How do I validate query parameters with regex in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use Query() with pattern parameter: name: str = Query(pattern="^[a-z]+$"). FastAPI uses Pydantic's Field with pattern for regex validation

user_query: How does FastAPI handle concurrency?
ground_truth: FastAPI uses async/await with ASGI for concurrent request handling. Synchronous endpoints run in a thread pool. Use async def endpoints for non-blocking I/O operations

user_query: How do I serve static files in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Mount StaticFiles with app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory="static"), name="static"). StaticFiles is from fastapi.staticfiles. Files in the directory are served at /static/filename

user_query: How do I create custom response types in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Subclass Response from starlette.responses and implement the render method. Or use the existing response types: JSONResponse, HTMLResponse, PlainTextResponse, RedirectResponse, FileResponse, StreamingResponse

user_query: What is the difference between async def and def in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use async def for asynchronous endpoints (non-blocking I/O). Use def for synchronous endpoints (CPU-bound or blocking). FastAPI runs def endpoints in a thread pool to avoid blocking the event loop

user_query: How do I add authentication to FastAPI with JWT?
ground_truth: Create /token endpoint that validates credentials and returns access_token and token_type. Use python-jose or PyJWT for token encoding/decoding. Protect routes with Depends(get_current_user) dependency

user_query: How do I handle large file uploads in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use UploadFile which streams the file to disk instead of loading into memory. Set max file size in middleware or proxy. Monitor server disk space for large concurrent uploads

user_query: How do I add WebSocket authentication in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Check authentication during the WebSocket handshake by inspecting cookies or query params in the websocket endpoint before calling accept(). Reject with websocket.close(code=4001) on failure

user_query: How do I configure CORS for WebSocket connections?
ground_truth: WebSocket connections are not subject to CORS in the same way as HTTP. The browser's same-origin policy for WebSockets is handled via the Origin header. Validate the Origin header server-side

user_query: How do I create a health check endpoint in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Create a simple GET endpoint at /health that returns {"status": "ok"}. Optionally check database connectivity and other dependencies. Exclude from OpenAPI docs with include_in_schema=False

user_query: What is Pydantic v2 compatibility with FastAPI?
ground_truth: FastAPI 0.100+ supports Pydantic v2. Key changes: model_config instead of Config class, field_validator replaces @validator, model_validator replaces @root_validator. FastAPI handles both v1 and v2 models

user_query: How does FastAPI compare to Django REST Framework?
ground_truth: FastAPI is async-native, faster, has automatic OpenAPI docs, and is more minimal. DRF has more built-in features (admin, ORM, serializers), more mature ecosystem, and synchronous by default

user_query: How do I handle request timeouts in FastAPI?
ground_truth: FastAPI doesn't have built-in request timeout. Use uvicorn's timeout_keep_alive and timeout_notify settings. For per-route timeouts, implement asyncio.wait_for or use a reverse proxy timeout

user_query: How do I use environment variables in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use pydantic-settings to define a Settings class with BaseSettings. FastAPI supports loading from .env files. Access via settings object defined as a module-level singleton or dependency

user_query: How do I add API versioning in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use APIRouter with prefix="/v1" and prefix="/v2". Or mount sub-applications. Or use header-based versioning. The APIRouter approach is simplest: organize routes by version

user_query: How do I debug FastAPI applications?
ground_truth: Use uvicorn --reload for auto-restart on changes. Use Python debugger with breakpoints. Enable debug logging with --log-level debug. Use middleware to log request/response timing

user_query: How do I create a middleware that measures request time in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use @app.middleware("http") to capture time before and after the request. Record start_time = time.time(), call await call_next(request), then log elapsed = time.time() - start_time

user_query: How do I handle JSON serialization of datetime objects in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Pydantic handles datetime serialization automatically. Use datetime fields in BaseModel. Custom encoders can be passed via json_encoders in model_config or via FastAPI's default response encoder

user_query: How do I set up CORS for multiple domains in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Pass a list of allowed origins to CORSMiddleware: allow_origins=["https://example.com", "https://app.example.com"]. Use allow_origin_regex for pattern matching multiple subdomains

user_query: How does FastAPI generate OpenAPI schemas?
ground_truth: FastAPI uses Pydantic models to generate JSON Schema for request/response bodies. Route decorators provide additional metadata (tags, summary, description, response_model). The schema is available at /openapi.json

user_query: How do I use FastAPI with GraphQL?
ground_truth: FastAPI can serve GraphQL using strawberry-graphql or graphene. Integrate as a mounted ASGI app or a route handler. Example: app.mount("/graphql", GraphQLApp(schema=graphene.Schema(query=Query)))

user_query: How do I implement refresh tokens in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Issue both access_token (short-lived, ~15 min) and refresh_token (long-lived, ~7 days) at login. Create /refresh endpoint that validates refresh_token and issues new access_token. Store refresh tokens securely

user_query: How do I handle database migrations with FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use Alembic for SQLAlchemy migrations. Initialize with alembic init alembic. Generate migrations with alembic revision --autogenerate. Apply with alembic upgrade head. Run migrations at app startup or via CLI

user_query: How do I add rate limiting per user in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Use a dependency that checks request count per user_id from Redis or database. Use token bucket or sliding window algorithm. FastAPI doesn't have built-in rate limiting; implement as middleware or dependency

user_query: What are the breaking changes from FastAPI 0.95 to 0.100?
ground_truth: Pydantic v2 support was added, which changes model validation behavior. The jsonable_encoder behavior changed for some edge cases. Deprecated parameters were removed. Lifespan protocol was stabilized

user_query: How do I create custom OpenAPI tags with descriptions in FastAPI?
ground_truth: Define openapi_tags parameter when creating FastAPI app: openapi_tags=[{"name": "users", "description": "User operations"}]. Then use tags=["users"] on route decorators

user_query: How do I use FastAPI with SQLAlchemy async sessions?
ground_truth: Use SQLAlchemy's async engine with create_async_engine. Create async sessions with AsyncSession. Use async def endpoints and await session.execute(). Use async_scoped_session for request-scoped sessions

user_query: How does FastAPI handle request body size limits?
ground_truth: FastAPI doesn't have a built-in request body size limit. Use a reverse proxy (Nginx, Caddy) or ASGI middleware to limit body size. For multipart uploads, UploadFile streams to disk without size limit
